paper_title: Pollen Germination and Temperature Tolerance Analysis

methods.method_name: Pollen Germination and Pollen Tube Growth Measurement
methods.description: This method measures pollen germination and pollen tube growth by observing germinated pollen under a microscope, photographing, and measuring pollen tube lengths to determine germination percentage and average tube length.

steps.description: Petri dishes with germinated pollen were observed under a microscope at a 10× magnification power.
Ten microscopic fields per Petri dish were photographed randomly.
Pollen germination percentage was determined by counting 200 pollen grains from the randomly photographed images.
Using an ocular micrometer mounted on the microscope's eyepiece, pollen tube length was measured and converted to micrometers after calibration with a stage micrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen tube length using micrometer

steps.description: The average was used to analyze the tube length.
The Eq. 1 was used to compute the percentage of the germinated pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: compute germinated pollen percentage using equation

methods.method_name: Principal Component Analysis for Temperature Tolerance
methods.description: This method uses principal component analysis (PCA) to identify key pollen parameters and classify apple cultivars based on their tolerance to low and high temperatures.

steps.description: PCA was used to identify the key pollen parameters in this investigation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA to identify key pollen parameters

steps.description: Eight PCAs were generated using values of maximum pollen germination percentage, pollen tube growth, and the cardinal temperatures.
Three PCs with eigenvalues > 1 contributed 85.94% of the total variation among the cultivars.
PC1 and PC3 were used to identify cold tolerant cultivars.
steps.searchable_summary: use PC1 and PC3 to identify cold tolerant cultivars

steps.description: PC1 and PC2 were used to identify high-temperature tolerant cultivars.
steps.searchable_summary: use PC1 and PC2 to identify high-temperature tolerant cultivars

steps.description: Classifications were conducted using the maximum pollen germination percentage, maximum pollen tube growth, and cardinal temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: classify cultivars based on pollen parameters and cardinal temperature

methods.method_name: Curve Estimation and Fit Model Selection
methods.description: This method involves using linear and nonlinear regression models to evaluate pollen germination percentage and pollen tube length, selecting the best-fit model for developmental response to temperature.

steps.description: Linear and nonlinear regression models were used to evaluate the maximum pollen germination percentage and pollen tube length.
steps.searchable_summary: apply regression models to evaluate pollen parameters

steps.description: The best-fit model was determined by applying quadratic, cubic, or higher-order polynomials and modified bilinear equations.
steps.searchable_summary: determine best-fit model using polynomials and bilinear equations

steps.description: The modified bilinear model was the most appropriate and best fit for this data.
steps.searchable_summary: select modified bilinear model as best fit

steps.description: The optimum temperature was calculated using SAS and the modified Gauss-Newton method.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate optimum temperature using SAS and Gauss-Newton method

steps.description: Modified bilinear equations were applied to compute pollen germination percentage, pollen tube growth, and maximum and minimum temperatures.
steps.searchable_summary: apply modified bilinear equations to compute pollen parameters
